title: A giant warm wave is crossing the Pacific, signaling an El Niño that could alter weather worldwide this year
content: Waves of higher, warmer water move eastward across the Pacific Ocean a few months before an El Niño emerges. Several have shown up in 2026 satellite data.
snippet: Waves of higher, warmer water move eastward across the Pacific Ocean a few months before an El Niño emerges. Several have shown up in 2026 satellite data.

title: Fields of thunder Scientists track processes that fuel powerful thunderstorms over U.S. Corn Belt
content: An international team of scientists has demonstrated how powerful thunderstorm complexes over the U.S. Corn Belt are fueled by moisture rising from the region’s fertile fields or just beneath them.
snippet: An international team of scientists has demonstrated how powerful thunderstorm complexes over the U.S. Corn Belt are fueled by moisture rising from the region’s fertile fields or just beneath them.

title: ‘There is no great master plan’: anxiety as UK homes, roads and railways sink into the sea
content: Increasing coastal erosion has hit communities’ livelihoods and put lifestyles under threat.
snippet: Increasing coastal erosion has hit communities’ livelihoods and put lifestyles under threat.

title: Call for input: Hazards media toolkit pilot
content: Help shape a new pilot project by selecting priority hazards and recommending experts for the Technical Working Group. The survey takes 5 to 7 minutes and all responses are confidential.
snippet: Help shape a new pilot project by selecting priority hazards and recommending experts for the Technical Working Group. The survey takes 5 to 7 minutes and all responses are confidential.

title: Climate change exposes hundreds of millions to longer and deadlier pre-monsoon heat in South Asia
content: From mid April and advancing into May 2026, India and Pakistan experienced extremely high temperatures, including daily maximum temperatures above 46°C in many cities in India ( Times of India, 2026 ).
snippet: From mid April and advancing into May 2026, India and Pakistan experienced extremely high temperatures, including daily maximum temperatures above 46°C in many cities in India ( Times of India, 2026 )

title: Calls for Proposals: Towards evidence-based decision making and risk investment for disaster risk reduction and early warning in Eastern Africa
content: A grant to upgrade the Kenya national risk profile using the probabilistic approach and conduct DRR integrated fiscal risk analysis and recommendations.
snippet: A grant to upgrade the Kenya national risk profile using the probabilistic approach and conduct DRR integrated fiscal risk analysis and recommendations.

title: Associate Programme Management Officer, P2, Panama City
content: This project position reports to the Economic Affairs Officer under the overall authority of the Deputy and Chief of the Regional Office for Americas and the Caribbean in Panama.
snippet: This project position reports to the Economic Affairs Officer under the overall authority of the Deputy and Chief of the Regional Office for Americas and the Caribbean in Panama.

title: Extreme flood events as a neglected natural hazard for marginalized Roma communities of Slovakia
content: Marginalized Roma communities (MRCs) located in flood prone areas represent a highly pressing research challenge.
snippet: Marginalized Roma communities (MRCs) located in flood prone areas represent a highly pressing research challenge.

title: Disaster risk in Brazil: trends, challenges and policy insights
content: The study analyzes the evolution of DRR policies in Brazil and examines how these developments are reflected in disaster outcomes.
snippet: The study analyzes the evolution of DRR policies in Brazil and examines how these developments are reflected in disaster outcomes.

title: A novel dynamic disaster risk assessment of urban built environments: An application to flood and earthquake
content: This study presents a dynamic disaster risk assessment methodology for urban built environments, integrating flood and earthquake scenarios with spatiotemporal patterns of population exposure and vulnerability.
snippet: This study presents a dynamic disaster risk assessment methodology for urban built environments, integrating flood and earthquake scenarios with spatiotemporal patterns of population exposure and vuln

title: Europe and Central Asia Regional Platform for Disaster Risk Reduction
content: The next Europe and Central Asia Regional Platform for Disaster Risk Reduction (RP2027) will take place 25–27 May 2027 in Bucharest, Romania, hosted by the Government of Romania.
snippet: The next Europe and Central Asia Regional Platform for Disaster Risk Reduction (RP2027) will take place 25–27 May 2027 in Bucharest, Romania, hosted by the Government of Romania.

title: Disaster risk planning in an evolving risk landscape: Barriers and enablers in the integration of land use and preparedness actors
content: This study explored barriers and enablers in collaborative planning within an evolving risk landscape. In this context, friction-free, bidirectional feedback between the preventive and the reactive parts of the DRM system-of-systems is essential.
snippet: This study explored barriers and enablers in collaborative planning within an evolving risk landscape. In this context, friction-free, bidirectional feedback between the preventive and the reactive pa

title: Women's well-being and everyday adaptation to heat
content: This review examines how extreme heat affects women’s well-being and highlights everyday adaptation practices across Africa, Asia and Oceania.
snippet: This review examines how extreme heat affects women’s well-being and highlights everyday adaptation practices across Africa, Asia and Oceania.

title: New ICT toolkit for integrated climate change assessment developed by EU-funded NEVERMORE project
content: The EU-funded NEVERMORE project has developed an ICT toolkit, which combines climate science, socio-economic data, and stakeholder knowledge to promote integrated climate change assessment.
snippet: The EU-funded NEVERMORE project has developed an ICT toolkit, which combines climate science, socio-economic data, and stakeholder knowledge to promote integrated climate change assessment.

title: Wildfire risk is now spreading to cool climates like the Scottish Highlands and Irish uplands
content: The most destructive wildfire season on record in Europe was in 2025, with more than one million hectares burned and tens of thousands of people displaced by fires across the continent.
snippet: The most destructive wildfire season on record in Europe was in 2025, with more than one million hectares burned and tens of thousands of people displaced by fires across the continent.

title: The increasing risk of heatwaves to vulnerable populations in eastern Australia
content: This study investigates historical trends in heatwave characteristics across New South Wales (NSW), identifies regions with increasing exposure in recent decades, and assesses risks to vulnerable populations using a combined heatwave risk framework.
snippet: This study investigates historical trends in heatwave characteristics across New South Wales (NSW), identifies regions with increasing exposure in recent decades, and assesses risks to vulnerable popu

title: In Bihar, groundwater treatment units were installed in regions that didn’t need them
content: Arsenic-contaminated groundwater affects more than 230 million people living in 108 countries. About 180 million of these people live in the Indian subcontinent (which includes Bangladesh, Nepal, and Pakistan, in addition to India) and Southeast Asia.
snippet: Arsenic-contaminated groundwater affects more than 230 million people living in 108 countries. About 180 million of these people live in the Indian subcontinent (which includes Bangladesh, Nepal, and

title: Climate change crisis: IMD to change parameters to declare heatwaves
content: The India Meteorological Department will soon revise the criteria for declaring heatwave conditions in the country, as the present parameters do not suit India's geographical conditions, according to official sources here.

title: Coupling land use planning and multi-stakeholder dynamics to inform disaster risk management: Who pays for risk and who gains from intervention?
content: This study assesses how land use and acquisition strategies shape disaster losses, government expenditures, and household outcomes over time.
snippet: This study assesses how land use and acquisition strategies shape disaster losses, government expenditures, and household outcomes over time.

title: As Ebola resurfaces in DR Congo, so do critical questions about how to respond
content: A large-scale response has begun to an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo that went undetected for up to two months and has already become one of the largest on record, with 671 suspected cases and 160 suspected deaths.
snippet: A large-scale response has begun to an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo that went undetected for up to two months and has already become one of the largest on record, with 671 su

title: US exposed to $375 billion-$1 trillion in aggregated uninsured flood losses from a range of extreme events
content: US residential flood exposure poses a growing credit risk, given large insurance protection gap.
snippet: US residential flood exposure poses a growing credit risk, given large insurance protection gap.

title: When resilience is built for everyone, growth and stability follow
content: Reaching every individual before the next flood, drought or economic shock requires collaboration across financial institutions, policy-makers, development organizations and the private sector.
snippet: Reaching every individual before the next flood, drought or economic shock requires collaboration across financial institutions, policy-makers, development organizations and the private sector.

title: Wildfire season arrives again: Is Europe ready this time?
content: Early fires in the Netherlands and Czechia offered a first test of EU emergency coordination
snippet: Early fires in the Netherlands and Czechia offered a first test of EU emergency coordination

title: From volcanic vents to safer skies
content: Improved estimates of Eruption Source Parameters can sharpen forecasts of volcanic plume rise and ash dispersal, supporting aviation safety and hazard response.
snippet: Improved estimates of Eruption Source Parameters can sharpen forecasts of volcanic plume rise and ash dispersal, supporting aviation safety and hazard response.

title: Global human population pushing Earth past breaking point
content: The Earth has already exceeded its ability to support the global population sustainably, with new research warning of increasing pressure on food security, climate stability, and human wellbeing.
snippet: The Earth has already exceeded its ability to support the global population sustainably, with new research warning of increasing pressure on food security, climate stability, and human wellbeing.

title: 2026 World Cup stadiums: Extreme heat rising
content: Nearly all of the 2026 World Cup host stadiums now see more extremely hot days during the tournament period (June-July) than during the first North American World Cup in 1970.
snippet: Nearly all of the 2026 World Cup host stadiums now see more extremely hot days during the tournament period (June-July) than during the first North American World Cup in 1970.

title: Nagpur Institute of Technology
content: Nagpur Institute of Technology provides quality technical education, promoting research and innovation, and developing socially responsible professionals who contribute towards sustainable development, safety awareness, and community resilience
snippet: Nagpur Institute of Technology provides quality technical education, promoting research and innovation, and developing socially responsible professionals who contribute towards sustainable development

title: Early-warning engineering for corridor vulnerability: A remote sensing threshold model for disaster risk reduction in Tanzania
content: This research article examines how Earth observation technologies can strengthen early warning and disaster risk reduction for transport infrastructure in Tanzania.
snippet: This research article examines how Earth observation technologies can strengthen early warning and disaster risk reduction for transport infrastructure in Tanzania.

title: Africa has been managing climate volatility for decades – what the rest of the world can learn from it
content: The United States’ retreat from aspects of global climate and health leadership has exposed the risk Africa took in relying on external funding for these projects.
snippet: The United States’ retreat from aspects of global climate and health leadership has exposed the risk Africa took in relying on external funding for these projects.

title: Climate change could significantly worsen summer air quality in future decades
content: Across the world, air pollution is associated with more early deaths than any other environmental exposure, raising risks of dying from lung cancer, respiratory infection, heart and lung disease and other causes.

title: Summers are getting hotter and deadlier, especially for older Americans
content: Heat waves are striking in surprising places where milder temperatures were once the norm﻿
snippet: Heat waves are striking in surprising places where milder temperatures were once the norm

title: Breaking the cycle of urban vulnerability: pathways to resilient cities
content: Every year, disasters cost the global economy USD 202 billion, but when cascading impacts on education, supply chains and health are considered, the true cost rises to USD 2.3 trillion.
snippet: Every year, disasters cost the global economy USD 202 billion, but when cascading impacts on education, supply chains and health are considered, the true cost rises to USD 2.3 trillion.

title: Nature-Based solutions in São Tomé and Príncipe
content: A World Bank Group-financed coastal road project demonstrates how nature‑based solutions can protect foundational transport infrastructure and sustain economic activity in São Tomé and Príncipe.
snippet: A World Bank Group-financed coastal road project demonstrates how nature‑based solutions can protect foundational transport infrastructure and sustain economic activity in São Tomé and Príncipe.

title: Is New Zealand’s grazing system still fit for a warming climate?
content: The science underpinning Aotearoa New Zealand’s dairy, beef and sheep grazing systems was largely established from the 1950s onward, but new analysis shows that the climate those systems were built for has shifted significantly.

title: Tropical cyclones give rise to unique type of heat wave in Japan
content: “Moist heat wave” identified as responsible for a quarter of heat wave days
snippet: “Moist heat wave” identified as responsible for a quarter of heat wave days

title: Rice feeds billions of people – but its role in fueling climate change is growing
content: Rice feeds more than half the world. It underpins daily meals for billions of people. But the same flooded soils that help rice thrive also create ideal conditions for microbes that release climate-warming gases.
snippet: Rice feeds more than half the world. It underpins daily meals for billions of people. But the same flooded soils that help rice thrive also create ideal conditions for microbes that release climate-wa

title: Are earthquake swarms along the Cotabato Trench echoes of a 1976 magnitude 8.1 quake?
content: Scientists explore recent earthquake swarms off the shores of Mindanao, a southern Philippine island, and consider similarities to the devastating 1976 Moro Gulf earthquake.
snippet: Scientists explore recent earthquake swarms off the shores of Mindanao, a southern Philippine island, and consider similarities to the devastating 1976 Moro Gulf earthquake.

title: Early warning systems are key to minimizing disasters
content: A study of one of the world’s longest-running disaster warning systems – desert locust monitoring – finds surveillance limits damages and generates returns of up to 680 times the investment.
snippet: A study of one of the world’s longest-running disaster warning systems – desert locust monitoring – finds surveillance limits damages and generates returns of up to 680 times the investment.

title: When drought did not win: disaster risk financing ignites hope in Mozambique’s Magude province
content: As of October 2025, 6,728 people in Magude have benefited from improved water access, including 2,561 women. Approximately 8,797 cattle have benefited from improved water access, and 1,045 households received food assistance during drought periods.
snippet: As of October 2025, 6,728 people in Magude have benefited from improved water access, including 2,561 women. Approximately 8,797 cattle have benefited from improved water access, and 1,045 households

title: Programmatic responses to extreme heat - guidance for humanitarian practitioners
content: A comprehensive, sector-by-sector practical guide helping humanitarian organizations understand, prepare for, and respond to the rapidly growing threat of extreme heat and heatwaves.
snippet: A comprehensive, sector-by-sector practical guide helping humanitarian organizations understand, prepare for, and respond to the rapidly growing threat of extreme heat and heatwaves.

title: Passive cooling for public buildings - guidance for humanitarian practitioners
content: A practical, non-technical guide helping humanitarian workers keep health centres and schools cooler through low-cost, energy-free building strategies suited to hot climates.
snippet: A practical, non-technical guide helping humanitarian workers keep health centres and schools cooler through low-cost, energy-free building strategies suited to hot climates.
